The surveyor tool, also known as a surgical instrument, is commonly utilized by dentists during implant surgery planning. It helps identify anatomical landmarks such as bone contours which can guide where an implant should be placed precisely. The purpose here seems aligned with ensuring proper fitment and function post-implant placement rather than purely aesthetic considerations like option B suggests.
